message:
feat(html): Complete HTML export module with encryption and test infrastructure

Major expansion of the html_export module for self-contained session exports:

Encryption (Web Crypto compatible):
- Add PBKDF2 key derivation for password-based encryption
- Implement AES-GCM encryption matching browser Web Crypto API
- Support optional encryption with graceful degradation
- Add pbkdf2 dependency for key derivation

Template & Rendering:
- Complete HtmlExporter with full template generation
- Add sophisticated CSS with dark/light theme support
- Implement message rendering with syntax highlighting
- Add search, filtering, and navigation JavaScript
- Support tool invocation collapsible sections

Filename Generation:
- Smart cross-platform filename sanitization
- Configurable components (date, agent, project)
- Max length truncation with word boundaries

Test Infrastructure:
- Add Node.js test harness for encryption compatibility
- Create test fixtures with edge cases and real sessions
- Add package.json for JS test dependencies

Enable 'encryption' feature by default in Cargo.toml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@ +{"sessionId":"unicode-001","workspace":"/home/dev/project","agent":"claude_code","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"日本語でコードを書いてください。变量名を日本語にしてほしいです。"},"uuid":"msg-001","timestamp":"2026-01-15T10:00:00.000Z"} +{"sessionId":"unicode-001","workspace":"/home/dev/project","agent":"claude_code","type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"はい、日本語の変数名でコードを書きます： ```python # 日本語変数名のサンプル 合計金額 = 0 商品リスト = ["りんご", "バナナ", "オレンジ"] for 商品 in 商品リスト: 単価 = 価格を取得(商品) 合計金額 += 単価 print(f"合計: ¥{合計金額}") ``` 中文变量名示例： ```python # 中文变量名 总金额 = 0 商品列表 = ["苹果", "香蕉", "橙子"] for 商品 in 商品列表: 单价 = 获取价格(商品) 总金额 += 单价 print(f"总计: ¥{总金额}") ```"},"uuid":"msg-002","timestamp":"2026-01-15T10:00:30.000Z"} +{"sessionId":"unicode-001","workspace":"/home/dev/project","agent":"claude_code","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"Emoji test! 🎉🚀💻🔥 Let's add some mathematical symbols: ∫∑∏√∞ ≤≥≠≈ ∀∃∈∉ α β γ δ ε"},"uuid":"msg-003","timestamp":"2026-01-15T10:01:00.000Z"} +{"sessionId":"unicode-001","workspace":"/home/dev/project","agent":"claude_code","type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"Great! Here's code with mathematical notation 📐: ```python import math # Mathematical operations 🔢 def calculate_integral(f, a, b, n=1000): """Approximate ∫f(x)dx from a to b""" Δx = (b - a) / n # Delta x Σ = sum(f(a + i * Δx) for i in range(n)) # Summation return Σ * Δx # Greek letters as variables α = 0.5 # alpha - learning rate β = 0.9 # beta - momentum γ = 0.99 # gamma - discount factor ε = 1e-8 # epsilon - small constant # Set theory operations A = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5} B = {4, 5, 6, 7, 8} # ∈ (element of) assert 3 ∈ A # SyntaxError in Python, use: 3 in A # ∪ (union), ∩ (intersection) print(f"A ∪ B = {A | B}") # Union print(f"A ∩ B = {A & B}") # Intersection # Infinity ∞ = float('inf') print(f"Is 1000000 < ∞? {1000000 < ∞}") # True ``` 🎯 Note: Python doesn't allow most Unicode math symbols as operators, but they work in strings and comments!"},"uuid":"msg-004","timestamp":"2026-01-15T10:01:30.000Z"} +{"sessionId":"unicode-001","workspace":"/home/dev/project","agent":"claude_code","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"مرحبا! هذا نص عربي من اليمين إلى اليسار. (Arabic RTL text test)"},"uuid":"msg-005","timestamp":"2026-01-15T10:02:00.000Z"} +{"sessionId":"unicode-001","workspace":"/home/dev/project","agent":"claude_code","type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"أهلاً وسهلاً! Here's how to handle RTL text in HTML: ```html

English text مع نص عربي mixed together.

``` CSS for RTL support: ```css [dir="rtl"] { text-align: right; direction: rtl; } /* Logical properties work with any direction */ .container { margin-inline-start: 1rem; /* Left in LTR, Right in RTL */ padding-inline-end: 1rem; /* Right in LTR, Left in RTL */ } ```"},"uuid":"msg-006","timestamp":"2026-01-15T10:02:30.000Z"} +{"sessionId":"unicode-001","workspace":"/home/dev/project","agent":"claude_code","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"Special characters test: & "quotes" 'apostrophes' \ backslash /forward/ `backticks`"},"uuid":"msg-007","timestamp":"2026-01-15T10:03:00.000Z"} +{"sessionId":"unicode-001","workspace":"/home/dev/project","agent":"claude_code","type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"That's a good test for HTML escaping! Here's how those characters should be handled: | Character | HTML Entity | Purpose | |-----------|-------------|--------| | `<` | `<` | Prevent tag injection | | `>` | `>` | Close tags | | `&` | `&` | Entity start | | `"` | `"` | Attribute quotes | | `'` | `'` | Attribute quotes (alternative) | The `')); // Output: <script>alert("XSS")</script> ```"},"uuid":"msg-008","timestamp":"2026-01-15T10:03:30.000Z"} +{"type":"summary","timestamp":"2026-01-15T10:04:00.000Z","summary":"Tested Unicode handling: Japanese, Chinese, emoji, mathematical symbols, Arabic RTL, Hebrew, and special HTML characters for XSS prevention."}

@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+{"sessionId":"codex-api-001","workspace":"/home/dev/api-service","agent":"codex","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"I need to design a REST API for a task management system. It should support users, projects, and tasks with proper relationships."},"uuid":"msg-001","timestamp":"2026-01-13T11:00:00.000Z"}
+{"sessionId":"codex-api-001","workspace":"/home/dev/api-service","agent":"codex","type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"I'll help you design a comprehensive REST API for task management. Let me outline the resources and their relationships:

## Resource Hierarchy

```
Users
  └── Projects (user owns projects)
        └── Tasks (project contains tasks)
              └── Comments (task has comments)
              └── Attachments (task has files)
```

## API Endpoints Overview

### Users
```
GET    /api/v1/users              # List users (admin)
GET    /api/v1/users/:id          # Get user
POST   /api/v1/users              # Create user (register)
PATCH  /api/v1/users/:id          # Update user
DELETE /api/v1/users/:id          # Delete user
GET    /api/v1/users/:id/projects # User's projects
```

### Projects
```
GET    /api/v1/projects           # List projects
GET    /api/v1/projects/:id       # Get project
POST   /api/v1/projects           # Create project
PATCH  /api/v1/projects/:id       # Update project
DELETE /api/v1/projects/:id       # Delete project
GET    /api/v1/projects/:id/tasks # Project tasks
```

### Tasks
```
GET    /api/v1/tasks              # List all tasks (with filters)
GET    /api/v1/tasks/:id          # Get task
POST   /api/v1/tasks              # Create task
PATCH  /api/v1/tasks/:id          # Update task
DELETE /api/v1/tasks/:id          # Delete task
POST   /api/v1/tasks/:id/assign   # Assign user to task
POST   /api/v1/tasks/:id/complete # Mark complete
```

Would you like me to detail the request/response schemas?"},"uuid":"msg-002","timestamp":"2026-01-13T11:01:00.000Z"}
+{"sessionId":"codex-api-001","workspace":"/home/dev/api-service","agent":"codex","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"Yes, please show me the OpenAPI schema for the Task resource."},"uuid":"msg-003","timestamp":"2026-01-13T11:02:00.000Z"}
+{"sessionId":"codex-api-001","workspace":"/home/dev/api-service","agent":"codex","type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"Here's a comprehensive OpenAPI 3.0 schema for the Task resource:

```yaml
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Task Management API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: RESTful API for managing tasks, projects, and users

paths:
  /api/v1/tasks:
    get:
      summary: List tasks
      tags: [Tasks]
      parameters:
        - name: project_id
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            format: uuid
          description: Filter by project
        - name: assignee_id
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            format: uuid
          description: Filter by assignee
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [todo, in_progress, review, done]
          description: Filter by status
        - name: priority
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [low, medium, high, urgent]
        - name: due_before
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            format: date
          description: Filter tasks due before date
        - name: page
          in: query
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 1
        - name: limit
          in: query
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 20
            maximum: 100
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Paginated list of tasks
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/TaskListResponse'
    
    post:
      summary: Create task
      tags: [Tasks]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/TaskCreate'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Task created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task'
        '400':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest'
        '422':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError'

  /api/v1/tasks/{id}:
    parameters:
      - name: id
        in: path
        required: true
        schema:
          type: string
          format: uuid
    
    get:
      summary: Get task by ID
      tags: [Tasks]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Task details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task'
        '404':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound'

    patch:
      summary: Update task
      tags: [Tasks]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/TaskUpdate'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Task updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task'

components:
  schemas:
    Task:
      type: object
      required:
        - id
        - title
        - status
        - project_id
        - created_at
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
          readOnly: true
        title:
          type: string
          minLength: 1
          maxLength: 200
        description:
          type: string
          maxLength: 5000
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [todo, in_progress, review, done]
          default: todo
        priority:
          type: string
          enum: [low, medium, high, urgent]
          default: medium
        project_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        assignee_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
          nullable: true
        due_date:
          type: string
          format: date
          nullable: true
        estimated_hours:
          type: number
          format: float
          minimum: 0
        tags:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
          maxItems: 10
        created_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          readOnly: true
        updated_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          readOnly: true

    TaskCreate:
      type: object
      required:
        - title
        - project_id
      properties:
        title:
          type: string
        description:
          type: string
        project_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        assignee_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        priority:
          type: string
          enum: [low, medium, high, urgent]
        due_date:
          type: string
          format: date
        tags:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string

    TaskUpdate:
      type: object
      properties:
        title:
          type: string
        description:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [todo, in_progress, review, done]
        priority:
          type: string
          enum: [low, medium, high, urgent]
        assignee_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        due_date:
          type: string
          format: date
        tags:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string

    TaskListResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        data:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task'
        pagination:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pagination'

    Pagination:
      type: object
      properties:
        page:
          type: integer
        limit:
          type: integer
        total:
          type: integer
        total_pages:
          type: integer

  responses:
    BadRequest:
      description: Bad request
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error'
    NotFound:
      description: Resource not found
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error'
    ValidationError:
      description: Validation error
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ValidationError'
```

This schema includes:
- Proper HTTP methods and status codes
- Pagination support
- Filtering parameters
- Request/response validation
- Error responses"},"uuid":"msg-004","timestamp":"2026-01-13T11:03:30.000Z"}
+{"sessionId":"codex-api-001","workspace":"/home/dev/api-service","agent":"codex","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"How should I handle authentication and authorization?"},"uuid":"msg-005","timestamp":"2026-01-13T11:04:00.000Z"}
+{"sessionId":"codex-api-001","workspace":"/home/dev/api-service","agent":"codex","type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"Great question! Here's a comprehensive auth strategy:

## Authentication Flow

```
┌─────────┐      ┌─────────┐      ┌─────────┐
│  Client │──1──>│   API   │──2──>│   Auth  │
│         │<──3──│ Gateway │<──4──│ Service │
└─────────┘      └─────────┘      └─────────┘
                       │
                       5
                       ▼
                 ┌─────────┐
                 │ Backend │
                 │ Service │
                 └─────────┘

1. Client sends credentials
2. Gateway forwards to Auth Service
3. Auth Service validates & returns JWT
4. Gateway caches token info
5. Subsequent requests validated at gateway
```

## JWT Structure

```json
{
  "header": {
    "alg": "RS256",
    "typ": "JWT",
    "kid": "key-2024-01"
  },
  "payload": {
    "sub": "user-uuid-here",
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "roles": ["user", "project-admin"],
    "permissions": [
      "tasks:read",
      "tasks:write",
      "projects:read"
    ],
    "org_id": "org-uuid-here",
    "iat": 1705142400,
    "exp": 1705146000,
    "jti": "unique-token-id"
  }
}
```

## API Security Headers

```yaml
components:
  securitySchemes:
    bearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: JWT
    
    apiKey:
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      name: X-API-Key
      description: For service-to-service calls

security:
  - bearerAuth: []
```

## Authorization Middleware (Go)

```go
type AuthMiddleware struct {
    jwtValidator *JWTValidator
    permissions  map[string][]string // endpoint -> required permissions
}

func (m *AuthMiddleware) Authorize(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
        // 1. Extract token
        token := extractBearerToken(r)
        if token == "" {
            respondError(w, 401, "missing authorization header")
            return
        }

        // 2. Validate JWT
        claims, err := m.jwtValidator.Validate(token)
        if err != nil {
            respondError(w, 401, "invalid token")
            return
        }

        // 3. Check permissions
        required := m.permissions[r.URL.Path]
        if !hasAllPermissions(claims.Permissions, required) {
            respondError(w, 403, "insufficient permissions")
            return
        }

        // 4. Add claims to context
        ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), "claims", claims)
        next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
    })
}
```

## Resource-Based Authorization

```go
// Check if user can access specific task
func (s *TaskService) canAccess(ctx context.Context, taskID string) error {
    claims := ctx.Value("claims").(*Claims)
    task, err := s.repo.GetByID(taskID)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }

    // Admin can access everything
    if slices.Contains(claims.Roles, "admin") {
        return nil
    }

    // Check project membership
    project, _ := s.projectRepo.GetByID(task.ProjectID)
    if !slices.Contains(project.MemberIDs, claims.Sub) {
        return ErrForbidden
    }

    return nil
}
```

Would you like me to implement the database models next?"},"uuid":"msg-006","timestamp":"2026-01-13T11:05:30.000Z"}
+{"sessionId":"codex-api-001","workspace":"/home/dev/api-service","agent":"codex","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"Yes, let's do the database schema."},"uuid":"msg-007","timestamp":"2026-01-13T11:06:00.000Z"}
+{"sessionId":"codex-api-001","workspace":"/home/dev/api-service","agent":"codex","type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"Here's the PostgreSQL schema with proper relationships and indexes:

```sql
-- Enable UUID extension
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";

-- Users table
CREATE TABLE users (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    avatar_url VARCHAR(500),
    role VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user'
        CHECK (role IN ('user', 'admin', 'moderator')),
    email_verified BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    last_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ
);

CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_role ON users(role);

-- Projects table
CREATE TABLE projects (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    owner_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    visibility VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'private'
        CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'team', 'public')),
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'
        CHECK (status IN ('active', 'archived', 'deleted')),
    settings JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_projects_owner ON projects(owner_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_projects_status ON projects(status) WHERE status != 'deleted';

-- Project members (many-to-many)
CREATE TABLE project_members (
    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    role VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member'
        CHECK (role IN ('viewer', 'member', 'admin', 'owner')),
    joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    PRIMARY KEY (project_id, user_id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_project_members_user ON project_members(user_id);

-- Tasks table
CREATE TABLE tasks (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    title VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'todo'
        CHECK (status IN ('todo', 'in_progress', 'review', 'done', 'cancelled')),
    priority VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium'
        CHECK (priority IN ('low', 'medium', 'high', 'urgent')),
    assignee_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    reporter_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    due_date DATE,
    estimated_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    actual_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    position INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- For ordering within status columns
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
);

CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_project ON tasks(project_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_assignee ON tasks(assignee_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_status ON tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_due_date ON tasks(due_date) WHERE due_date IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_project_status ON tasks(project_id, status);

-- Task tags (many-to-many)
CREATE TABLE tags (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    color VARCHAR(7) NOT NULL DEFAULT '#6B7280',  -- Hex color
    UNIQUE(project_id, name)
);

CREATE TABLE task_tags (
    task_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    tag_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tags(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    PRIMARY KEY (task_id, tag_id)
);

-- Comments table
CREATE TABLE comments (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    task_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    author_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    edited BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_comments_task ON comments(task_id);

-- Activity log (audit trail)
CREATE TABLE activity_log (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    entity_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,  -- 'task', 'project', 'comment'
    entity_id UUID NOT NULL,
    action VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,  -- 'created', 'updated', 'deleted', 'assigned'
    actor_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    changes JSONB,  -- {"field": {"old": X, "new": Y}}
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_activity_entity ON activity_log(entity_type, entity_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_activity_created ON activity_log(created_at DESC);

-- Trigger for updated_at
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    NEW.updated_at = NOW();
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER users_updated_at BEFORE UPDATE ON users
    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
CREATE TRIGGER projects_updated_at BEFORE UPDATE ON projects
    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
CREATE TRIGGER tasks_updated_at BEFORE UPDATE ON tasks
    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
```
